ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn components can compose settings forms and navigation, while controlled section ownership, dirty-state interception, before-unload cleanup, native reset, async save recovery, and typed destructive confirmation remain application work.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: The workspace guarantees profile, preferences, notifications, and security sections; supports controlled or uncontrolled section and dirty state; and layers independently removable unsaved-change and destructive-operation protection over native save/reset.

visualSignature.summary: The settings workbench uses literal white, an Ink navigation rail, strong hierarchy, a Green header seam, Violet selection/focus, restrained corners, touch-aware targets, logical responsiveness, and preference fallbacks.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Dirty section changes receive an explicit stay-or-discard prompt with focus placement, and page exit installs a warning only while protection and dirty state are active.

api.enableWhen: Set protectUnsavedChanges to true; protection activates only while dirty is true.

disabledBehavior.ui: No unsaved-change alertdialog or stay/discard controls are rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Section navigation occurs immediately even when the consumer marks the section dirty.
disabledBehavior.events: No beforeunload listener or navigation interception is installed.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No alertdialog, warning description, or extra focus stops enter the accessibility tree.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A consumer-owned account action remains unavailable until consequences are reviewed and exact confirmation text is entered.

disabledBehavior.ui: No account-action section, review, consequences, confirmation field, or destructive button is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Section navigation, save, reset, and dirty-state behavior remains unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: No destructive review state, abort controller, or onConfirm callback exists.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No destructive heading, list, field, alert, or focus stops enter the accessibility tree.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit axe coverage includes native navigation and forms, instance-scoped dialog labels, enhancement removal, read-only reset cancellation, current-page state and focus restoration, narrow RTL, reduced motion, forced colors, and target sizing; complete Risk Class 3 assistive-technology and manual evidence is not recorded.

interactionEvidence.summary: Focused Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit evidence covers enhancement removal, mutable and read-only dirty-state reset semantics, repeated-instance ARIA ownership, unsaved-dialog focus entry and restoration, destructive gating, RTL, reduced motion, forced colors, and narrow/touch presentation; custom-control, lifecycle, authorization, and manual assistive-technology matrices remain open.

parityEvidence.summary: Package output is intentionally not planned by the catalog. Deterministic generation keeps canonical source, native registry, and Shadcn payloads on the same consumer transform, while the exact-tarball Next.js and Vite record verifies source mode and the source-only package boundary.

blockers.summary: Dependency closure, custom-control dirty-state review, destructive authorization, security/privacy/legal/notification review, current manual assistive technology, public dogfooding, and Passport approval remain open.

maturityAssessment.rationale: The source workflow and focused evidence exist, but distribution, lifecycle, authorization, security, privacy, legal, browser/device, and manual assistive-technology gates remain incomplete.
